evals.prompt: Our API is returning 500 errors for all requests, started about 10 minutes ago
evals.expected_output: Should produce an investigating-phase update that names the API component, describes user impact (500 errors), states what's not affected, includes approximate start time in UTC, and commits to a next-update time.
assertions: Suggests next step: run skill again for 'identified' phase when cause is found

evals.prompt: We found the cause — a bad deployment at 14:30 UTC broke the webhook service. We're rolling back. Users can retry failed webhooks manually.
evals.expected_output: Should produce an identified-phase update explaining the root cause (bad deployment), the fix (rollback), the actionable workaround (retry manually), and an ETA or next-update commitment.
assertions: Explains root cause in plain language (bad deployment)
Includes actionable user guidance (retry failed webhooks)

evals.prompt: We rolled back the deployment and webhook delivery looks normal again. Want to post a monitoring update.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a monitoring-phase update stating the fix was deployed, what it was, that the team is monitoring, and how long they'll monitor before resolving.
assertions: Tells users if action is needed
Suggests next step: resolve when stable

evals.prompt: The webhook incident is over. Started 14:30 UTC, fixed by 15:15 UTC. Bad deployment caused webhook delivery failures for about 45 minutes. Around 2000 webhooks failed.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a resolved update with exact time window, root cause summary, impact quantification, and postmortem commitment.
assertions: Includes exact start and end times in UTC
Quantifies impact (2000 failed webhooks, 45 min duration)
Mentions whether a postmortem will follow

evals.prompt: Our dashboard is slow, help me write a status update
evals.expected_output: Should ask the user which phase they're in (investigating, identified, etc.) before writing the update, or default to investigating if the situation clearly suggests it.
assertions: Determines or asks about the current phase
Asks clarifying questions about impact scope
Does not produce a vague update without sufficient detail

evals.prompt: Write an investigating update for our API being down
evals.expected_output: Should read .agents/status-page-context.md and use the tone, component names, and severity levels defined there to write the update.
assertions: Uses component names from context (not generic names)
Matches the tone defined in context
Applies severity-appropriate update cadence from context

evals.prompt: Write an incident update — we don't have the context file set up yet
evals.expected_output: Should suggest running status-page-context skill first, but proceed if user wants to skip. Should still follow all principles without the context file.
assertions: Proceeds without it if user wants
Still follows all 6 principles (scope, impact, actionable, timestamps, next-update, resolved-summary)

evals.prompt: Write a postmortem for yesterday's outage
evals.expected_output: Should redirect the user to the postmortem skill instead of attempting to write a postmortem.
assertions: Points user to the postmortem skill
Offers to help with a resolved update if the incident isn't closed yet
